event: chest pain radiating to left and right arms
cardiac arrest with coronary angiography showing 90% stenosis of circumflex artery
percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty plus stent placement on left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex
second coronary angiography with stent placement on same vessels
minimal aortic valve opening with mild aortic insufficiency
severe reduction of right ventricle longitudinal and concentric function
intra-aortic balloon pump ineffective in unloading left heart
TEE demonstrated improvement in right ventricle, anterior septum, posterior wall movement
reduction of venous return on ECMO device via Hoffmann clamp
shifted to total left ventricular assistance
anticoagulation monitored through activated coagulation time every 2 hours
blood transfusions (4 units packed red blood cells)
renal failure (stage 3) requiring continuous renal replacement therapy
